title: Existence of nontrival $n$-harmonic maps via min-max methods
author_string: Dorian Martino, Katarzyna Mazowiecka, Armin Schikorra
abstract: For any $n \geq 3$ and any closed manifold $\mathcal{N}$ with $π_{n+k}(\mathcal{N}) \neq \{0\}$ for some $k \geq 0$, we establish the existence of nontrivial $n$-harmonic maps from $\mathbb{S}^n$ into $\mathcal{N}$. When $k\geq 1$, these maps naturally appear as bubbling limits of $p$-harmonic maps with $p > n$, obtained by min-max constructions in the limit $p \to n^+$.
